role: Chief of Staff to President Ahmadinejad (2009–2013); leader of the 'deviant current'
bio:
Esfandiar Rahim Mashaei was the closest political ally of President [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad) and a former intelligence officer. He served as head of the [Cultural Heritage, Handicrafts and Tourism Organization](/orgs/cultural-heritage-organization) from 2005 to 2009, then was briefly appointed First Vice President in July 2009 before [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) ordered his removal after just one week. Ahmadinejad instead made him Chief of Staff, a position he held until 2013.

Mashaei became the focal point of what conservative clerics labeled the 'deviant current' (جریان انحرافی) — a faction accused of promoting Iranian nationalism over Islamic identity and undermining clerical authority. His statements criticizing the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts) and the doctrine of velayat-e faqih infuriated the religious establishment. [Mohammad-Taqi Mesbah-Yazdi](/people/mohammad-taqi-mesbah-yazdi) and other senior clerics publicly denounced his influence over the president.

Mashaei's daughter married Ahmadinejad's son, further cementing their personal bond. He was barred by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) from running in the 2013 presidential election. In September 2018, a Tehran Revolutionary Court sentenced him to six and a half years in prison for crimes against national security, propaganda against the state, and insulting Islamic sanctities. Associates of Mashaei, including [Abbas Ghaffari](/people/abbas-ghaffari-exorcist), were arrested in 2011 on charges of sorcery.
editorial_notes: Born 16 November 1960. Former intelligence officer. Sentenced September 2018 to 6.5 years. Family tie to Ahmadinejad: his daughter married Ahmadinejad's son Mehdi.
